_doc: DC1 numeric puzzle codes that share ONE common scramble function: a single 4-digit keypad code table baked into DINO.exe .data (AOT-recompiled from the JP build, 8 identical copies) checked by a shared comparator, with an optional in-prose document that states the code as a glyph-token string. A randomizer scrambles a row by (a) writing the new digits to all 8 EXE copies and (b) rewriting the document glyph run to the same number. Decoded + CE-witnessed 2026-07-01/02; see docs/dc1/MGMT-OFFICE-SAFE-PUZZLE-DECODE.md 14/16/17.

keypad_code_table.region_select: comparator picks US half if byte[[PSX-scratch0]+0x9ab8] & 0x80 else JP half; this build runs JP

document_glyph_format.normal_glyph: (index << 1) | 0x8000  (high byte 0x80, low byte = index*2, always even)
document_glyph_format.language_independent: glyph indices identical across english/ french/ etc.; only the text differs
document_glyph_format.location: in the decompressed room RDT (type-7 LZSS entry, load 0x80100000); edit needs re-LZSS + package repack

family.note: no readable document mention found (Reverse% content)
no readable document mention found (Reverse% content)

rebirth_english_document_store._doc: Classic REbirth english install (ddraw.dll 3536384 B SHA256 249F1B8F...) injects English document text at runtime from per-room diff files in a 7z archive embedded as PE resource DATA/107 (file 0x295BC0, size 0xB9CE3, DataEntry 0x2949D8). Diff record = u24 destWord + u16 count + u8 pad0 + count*u32, applied as memcpy to loadedRDT + destWord*4; terminator dest 0xFFFFFF. AST* = US-region variant (byte[DINO.exe 0x6DD8F8]&0x80), ST* = JP. Digits are plain glyph tokens. Decoded 2026-07-10; sync lever RebirthTextPatcher SHIPPED + LIVE-CONFIRMED in-game same day (rebuilt archive appended as .dinotxt section, Copy-codec streams accepted by REbirth's extractor); see docs/reference/dc1/puzzle/REBIRTH-DDRAW-TEXT-STORE-RE.md.
rebirth_english_document_store.loader_dll_va: 0x100453F0 (CRC-named in-memory VFS from resource 107; no disk fallback for diff/)

ddk._doc: DDK door-decode word puzzles (STATIC-SCD-RE.md cont.61 §C, decoded 2026-07-12). A door's examine sub launches a NATIVE screen via SCD op58 subtype-3 (handler 0x429AD2); screen type 3 dispatches to the DDK FSM 0x4B17D0. FSM state 8 (0x4B182A) is the disc-pair gate: GetFlag(11, id) AND GetFlag(11, id+7) — Input disc AND Code disc both required, fail sets SetFlag(2,10,1). Pass leads to the word puzzle against the EXE record table; success sets SetFlag(2,9,1) (same protocol as the keypad comparators above), then the room script does the persistent SetFlag(9, lock). NOT the keypad table — separate subsystem, own randomizer lever if ever scrambled.

word_table.record_layout: 7 records x 56 bytes (grid / available-letters / answer fields; answer field of record 0 at VA 0x65D320)
word_table.record_order: disc index 0..6 = H, N, L, E, W, S, D

fsm.dispatcher_va: 0x4B17D0 (screen type 3 in all 12 per-stage handler tables)

excluded_other_formats._doc: Detected by the same scan but NOT part of the keypad-table common function; a randomizer for those needs a different lever.

id_card_fingerprint_panels.format: 5-key/2-row object type 0x1a, RAW-digit encoding (no +1), separate comparators 0x800eddc8/0x800edf98

key_chip_codes.format: 4-digit code shown in a document but the lock is a tile/slide puzzle, not the keypad table

examples: DDK word passwords (now decoded - see the 'ddk' section)
